Amended MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ** Mini-Minutes ** Part 1 of 2 **

PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Appearances via Court Call: Richard Lee of Selman Breitman LLP (415.979.0400) for defendant National Lumber & Supply. No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. No opposition filed. Plaintiff filed an amended notice changing the date of the motion from October 15, 2020 to October 20, 2020. The Court continued the motion to October 22, 2020.

The motion is granted under C.C.P. 36(e) only. Plaintiff failed to provide clear and convincing medical documentation showing substantial medical doubt of his survival beyond six months as required under C.C.P. 36(d). There is no signature under the Attending/Hospital Physician Authorization. (Donadio Declaration, Exhibit B.)

The trial date is February 8, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 19, 2021. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 4, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is January 22, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 5, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than November 20, 2020. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
** End of Part 1 ** Mini-minutes continue in Part 2 of 2 **

Plaintiff'S Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ** Mini-Minutes ** Part 2 of 2 **
PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No later than November 5, 2020, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than November 5, 2020, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for December 10, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 25, 2020. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 8, 2021. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by January 29, 2021 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, February 4, 2021 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed. Judge: Cynthia M. Lee, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (503/CML)
